chiefComplaints: Exertional angina (NYHA Class III) for the past 3 months, worsening over the last 2 weeks; progressive shortness of breath on mild exertion.
hpi: The patient is a 62-year-old male with a known history of Ischemic Heart Disease (IHD), post-PTCA to LAD in 2021. He presented with recurrent chest pain radiating to the left arm, exacerbated by minimal walking, relieved by sublingual nitrates. He has a history of long-standing systemic hypertension and Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD Stage III) with baseline serum creatinine of 2.1 mg/dL. Coronary Angiography (CAG) performed on 12/10/2024 revealed severe Triple Vessel Disease (TVD).
relevantClinicalFindings: Echocardiogram shows regional wall motion abnormalities (RWMA) in LAD territory, LVEF of 45%. CAG: LM 30%, LAD 90% osteo-proximal, LCx 80% mid-segment, RCA 85% diffuse disease. Serum Creatinine: 2.1 mg/dL, eGFR: 38 mL/min/1.73m2. Hb: 10.8 g/dL. Potassium: 4.8 mEq/L. Urine routine: 1+ albuminuria. ECG shows Q waves in V1-V3, ST depression in V5-V6.
additionalClinicalNotes: Nephrology consultation obtained: Dr. S. Menon recommended perioperative hydration protocol, avoidance of nephrotoxic drugs, and keeping post-operative hemodialysis standby. High-risk consent obtained for CABG under Cardiopulmonary Bypass (CPB) due to renal impairment.
duration: Pre-op admission: 2 days, Planned ICU stay post-op: 4 days, Ward stay: 4 days.
treatmentTakenSoFar: Tab. Aspirin 75mg OD, Tab. Clopidogrel 75mg OD (held for 5 days pre-op), Tab. Atorvastatin 40mg OD, Tab. Metoprolol XL 50mg OD, Tab. Cilnidipine 10mg BD, Tab. Torsemide 10mg OD.
reasonForHospitalisation: Surgical revascularization (Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting - CABG x 3 grafts) for Triple Vessel Disease in a patient with multiple high-risk comorbidities.

insurance.insurerName: Star Health & Allied Insurance Co Ltd

notes: High-complexity CABG case requiring active clinical justification for prolonged ICU stay and high-risk anesthetic clearance due to CKD Stage III and prior stent history.
realGap: TPAs frequently raise queries or deny/delay pre-auth for CABG in patients with CKD due to high-risk postoperative mortality and potential for emergency dialysis, requiring explicit documentation of nephrology clearance and dedicated ICU standby plans to justify the high-risk surgical package.
sourceReasoning: Under IRDAI Health Insurance Standardization Guidelines, insurers must not arbitrarily reject pre-authorization for planned major surgeries like CABG based on comorbidities if the patient has crossed the PED waiting period (here, 3 years vs 4 years policy age) and clear nephrology optimization/standby dialysis protocols are documented to mitigate surgical risk.
rawDocumentText:
METRO CARDIAC CARE & SUPER SPECIALITY HOSPITAL, KOCHI
PRE-AUTHORIZATION REQUEST & CLINICAL SUMMARY

Patient Name: Mr. Ramesh Chandran | Age: 62 Years | Gender: Male
IP No: IP/2024/99182 | UHID: MCC-448102
Admitting Consultant: Dr. K. Radhakrishnan, MD, DM (Cardiology)
Surgical Consultant: Dr. Mathew Paul, MS, MCh (CTVS)
Insurer: Star Health & Allied Insurance Co Ltd | Policy No: 10120034291880
TPA Card No: SH9188092

DIAGNOSIS:
1. Triple Vessel Disease (TVD) - Atherosclerotic Heart Disease (ICD-10: I25.1)
2. Long-standing Systemic Hypertension (uncontrolled on presentation)
3. Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) Stage III
4. Status Post PTCA (DES) to LAD in 2021

CHIEF COMPLAINTS:
Patient presented with recurrent episodes of exertional chest pain and retrosternal burning sensation (NYHA Class III angina) for the last 3 months, worsening significantly over the last 15 days. Associated with dyspnea on minimal exertion.

CLINICAL HISTORY & PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Patient is a known hypertensive for 12 years and has a history of CAD, post-PTCA to LAD in 2021 at our center. The current insurance policy is active since 2020. 
On Examination:
Pulse: 84/min, BP: 170/100 mmHg, SpO2: 95% on room air, RR: 18/min. Chest: Bilateral clear. CVS: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs.

INVESTIGATIONS:
- ECG: Q waves in V1-V3, ST segment depressions in V5-V6, I, aVL.
- Echocardiogram: Regional wall motion abnormalities in LAD territory, LVEF: 45%, mild MR.
- Coronary Angiogram (12/10/2024): Left Main (LM) 30% plaque, LAD 90% osteo-proximal stenosis, LCx 80% mid-segment stenosis, RCA 85% diffuse disease. Severe Triple Vessel Disease (TVD).
- Renal Function: Blood Urea: 58 mg/dL, Serum Creatinine: 2.1 mg/dL, eGFR: 38 mL/min/1.73m2 (CKD Stage III).
- Potassium: 4.8 mEq/L, Sodium: 139 mEq/L. Hb: 10.8 g/dL.

SPECIALIST CONSULTATIONS:
1. Nephrology Consultation (Dr. S. Menon, MD, DM): Reviewed patient. Creatinine elevated at 2.1 mg/dL. Advised strict perioperative fluid balance monitoring, avoidance of nephrotoxic agents, low-dose contrast during any necessary procedures, and to keep backup Hemodialysis / CRRT standby post-operatively in the CTVS ICU. Cleared for high-risk CABG.
2. Anesthetic Pre-op Assessment: Graded as ASA Class III due to severe TVD and Stage III CKD. Cleared with high-risk consent.

PROPOSED PROCEDURE:
Planned for Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG x 3 Grafts: LIMA to LAD, SVG to OM, SVG to RCA) under General Anesthesia on Cardiopulmonary Bypass (CPB).
Estimated Stay: 10 Days (including 4 days in CTVS ICU for invasive monitoring, renal perfusion monitoring, and cardiac stabilization).
Estimated Cost: INR 3,85,000/-
simulatedDenialReason: Pre-authorization request for CABG is kept on query. Please provide: 1. Complete medical records/discharge summary of previous PTCA (2021) to verify PED disclosure. 2. Justification from the treating Cardiothoracic Surgeon for planned 4-day ICU stay. 3. Detailed Nephrology clearance with eGFR monitoring protocol and Dialysis standby plan, as baseline creatinine is elevated at 2.1 mg/dL.

expectedExtraction.insurerName: Star Health & Allied Insurance Co Ltd

expectedAppealCitations: Known case of IHD post-PTCA to LAD (2021), with current policy inception in 2020, covering the 3-year PED waiting period.
Nephrology consultation by Dr. S. Menon advising perioperative hydration protocol and standby hemodialysis.
Severe Triple Vessel Disease (LAD 90%, LCx 80%, RCA 85%) establishing absolute medical necessity for CABG over medical management.
Anesthetic risk graded as ASA Class III due to combined cardiovascular and renal impairment (baseline creatinine 2.1 mg/dL, eGFR 38 mL/min/1.73m2).
